response:
The heavyweight boxer is famous for his incredible power punches.
His aggressive fighting style always keeps the crowd on the edge of their seats.
He faced a professional opponent in a series of highly anticipated matches.
The first match was a true test of his legendary punching power.
In that initial bout, he managed to land exactly one hundred and twenty power punches.
This number set a high standard for the rest of the series.
The second match arrived with even more excitement from the fans.
He decided to increase his output of power punches significantly.
He increased his number of power punches by twenty-five percent in the second fight.
To find the new total, we must calculate twenty-five percent of one hundred and twenty.
Twenty-five percent of one hundred and twenty is equal to thirty power punches.
Adding those thirty new punches to the original one hundred and twenty gives us one hundred and fifty.
So, in the second match, he landed a total of one hundred and fifty power punches.
The third match was the final and most crucial encounter of the series.
He pushed himself even harder during this intense third battle.
He managed to land thirty more power punches than he did in the second match.
Since he landed one hundred and fifty in the second match, we add thirty to that number.
This calculation results in one hundred and eighty power punches for the third match.
Now we have the specific counts for all three individual fights.
The first match had one hundred and twenty power punches.
The second match had one hundred and fifty power punches.
The third match had one hundred and eighty power punches.
To find the total, we must add these three numbers together.
One hundred and twenty plus one hundred and fifty equals two hundred and seventy.
Adding the one hundred and eighty from the third match to two hundred and seventy gives us the final sum.
The total sum of power punches across all three matches is four hundred and fifty.
This impressive total demonstrates his consistent dominance in the ring.
His power punches were the main reason for his success in this series.
The crowd was left in awe by his sheer volume of impactful strikes.
Such a high number of power punches is rare in professional boxing history.
It proves that his aggressive style is highly effective against skilled opponents.
The boxer has truly cemented his legacy with this outstanding performance.
